page: Microsoft Azure DC4s_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This CPU provides 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz, supported by a 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 205 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Performance benchmarks place the DC4s_v2 in the average tier for most compute tasks. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield average results, as do Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores. However, the server demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and Passmark memory tests. Conversely, uncached large-block memory bandwidth is weak, with read speeds at 33.52 GB/sec and write speeds at 16.42 GB/sec. CPU encryption performance is also relatively weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource density provide stable, predictable performance for standard workloads without the overhead of shared-core models. Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average database operation benchmarks, this server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure DC4s_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCSv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers balanced performance, characterized by strong single-threaded gzip compression and Passmark memory scores, alongside average multi-core compute and database operation metrics. However, it exhibits weaker uncached large-block memory bandwidth and encryption speeds. Qualitatively, its dedicated CPU allocation offers reliable resource density for steady-state workloads. The DC4s_v2 is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database tasks.

tagline: A dedicated four-core Intel Xeon virtual machine offering balanced memory and local storage for stable general-purpose workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory and 205 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
